_comment: Soldier flocking, Stage B of #32 (cosmetic). Visible in any battle with movement, so this reuses the bundled showcase auto-battle: regiments march in and wheel into contact, which is exactly when flocking shows. Each soldier mark now eases toward its formation slot (an arrival spring) while pushing off its neighbours (separation), so the block deforms and trails as the regiment advances or turns, then reforms into ranks when it halts. Rendered through two MultiMeshes per unit (body + outline) = 2 draw calls regardless of soldier count, replacing Stage A's per-soldier draw_circle pair. Purely a rendering change: mark positions are never fed back into the deterministic aggregate sim, so combat, morale and replays are untouched. Per-soldier combat (Stage C) is the follow-up.
caption: Soldier flocking — #32 Stage B (cosmetic): each man now eases toward his formation slot and pushes off his neighbours, so the block deforms and trails as the regiment marches and wheels, then reforms into ranks when it halts — no more rigid grid snapping. Rendered via 2 MultiMesh draw calls per unit (was 2 per soldier). Sim is untouched (positions never feed back; no RNG, no balance change). Per-soldier combat comes in Stage C.
